theme: Tiny automaton approximating a stream statistic
title: Firefly Census: a Tiny Automaton Counts Blinks
strategies: always-guess-zero: never look at the stream at all, report a constant 0 hit-count (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference)

saturating exact counter: use all s states as a plain 0..s-1 tally that increments on every hit symbol and clamps at s-1; exact while the true count stays under the budget, but frozen and useless once it doesn't

calibrated geometric-bucket counter: split the s-state budget into n_levels x P states (level, phase); P consecutive hits advance one level, and decoding out(level,phase)=P*(2^level-1)+phase*2^level gives bounded RELATIVE error across a range that grows exponentially in n_levels; choose n_levels/P from the instance's alphabet/filter/length fields so exact small counts stay exact and large counts stay in relative-error range instead of clamping

seeded local refinement: start from a geometric-bucket design and perturb the level/phase split or the modular hit-classification to reduce mean relative error, deterministically, using only the public instance fields
